claim: The KV cache in transformer models grows quadratically with sequence length, making long-context inference expensive; Power Retention replaces this with fixed-size memory
actionable: For long-context inference applications, evaluate Power Retention architectures as alternatives to standard attention-based transformers to reduce per-token cost growth

claim: Windowed attention used in many 'long context' models simply discards information from middle sections, causing degraded performance on past context vs attention-based models
actionable: When evaluating long-context models claiming 256k+ tokens, verify they use true full attention, not windowed attention which loses middle-context information

claim: With Power Retention, inference speed improves by ~100x at 64k token context lengths compared to Flash Attention due to both flop reduction and memory paging savings
actionable: For high-throughput inference with long contexts, Power Retention architectures can reduce latency and costs by an order of magnitude vs standard transformers

claim: Vidrial's just-in-time kernel sweeping allows empirical discovery of optimal GPU configurations for specific problem shapes, achieving 20-30% speedups vs pre-optimized kernels
actionable: Use JIT kernel optimization frameworks like Vidrial when implementing custom attention kernels to discover hardware-optimal configurations for your specific input shapes

claim: Converting pre-trained transformers to Power Retention requires only ~2 hours of fine-tuning (mid-training) per 3B model without full re-pretraining
actionable: To adopt Power Retention for existing models, budget mid-training compute (not full pretraining) which is tractable even for smaller labs with limited resources

claim: Fixed-size memory in retention architectures simplifies GPU allocation and scheduling compared to dynamic KV caches, improving cluster utilization
actionable: For multi-user inference systems, retention-based architectures eliminate KV cache scheduling complexity by providing predictable memory footprint per user
